# Microsoft Pixel/Voxel Dark Deck

A reusable visual design system for building or restyling PowerPoint decks that
match the Microsoft "pixel/voxel" brand look: a confident black canvas, big bold
Segoe typography, and playful multi-colour pixel-block motifs (a city skyline, a
checker fade) used as accents.

Use this skill whenever the user wants slides to look like the two reference
screenshots in `assets/` (a hero "SELECT / ACT 01" title and a "What you want"
pillar row with a green outcome bar), or asks to apply this brand to an existing
deck generated by `presentation/generate_pptx.py`.

> The thin color-swatch strip visible on the right edge of the reference
> screenshots is a screen contrast-checker tool — it is **not** part of the
> slide. Ignore it.

---

## Design tokens

### Canvas & type
| Token | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Background | Pure black `#000000` |
| Aspect ratio | 16:9 (13.333 × 7.5 in) |
| Primary text | White `#FFFFFF` |
| Secondary / italic text | Light gray `#B3B3B3` |
| Footer / muted | Gray `#808080` |
| Font family | **Segoe UI** (Light for hero, Semibold/Bold for emphasis) |
| Hero word | Segoe UI **Bold**, very large (90–150 pt), centered |
| Eyebrow label | Segoe UI Semibold, ~13 pt, ALL CAPS, letter-spaced, accent colour |
| Section title | Segoe UI Semibold, 28–40 pt |
| Pillar sub-label | Segoe UI *Italic*, ~14 pt, gray |

### Accent palette (vibrant, rotate through these)
| Name | Hex |
| --- | --- |
| Cyan | `#50E6FF` |
| Lime green (outcome bar) | `#8CC63F` |
| Gold | `#FFB900` |
| Coral / red | `#FF6A4D` |
| Lavender | `#B4A0FF` |
| Blue | `#2899F5` |
| Magenta | `#E3008C` |
| PASS green | `#6BB700` |

Dark UI surfaces: panel `#141414`, panel-alt `#1E1E1E`, hairline `#3A3A3A`.
Text on the lime outcome bar is dark green `#203A00`.

---

## Slide archetypes

1. **Hero / title** (reference 1): black canvas; a small centered ALL-CAPS
   eyebrow above a large bold centered hero title; optional subtitle and meta
   lines below; a **pixel-block skyline** spanning the full width at the bottom.

2. **Pillars / value row** (reference 2): centered title at top; a horizontal
   row of *N* pillars, each = an accent-coloured icon, a white label, and an
   italic gray sub-label; **white circular arrow connectors** between pillars; a
   full-width **lime outcome bar** with a pixel-fade at its right end.

3. **Content** (bullets / tables): black canvas; centered title with a short
   centered accent underline; white bullets using vibrant square glyphs whose
   colour rotates per line; dark-zebra tables with an accent header row and
   PASS values in green; a small pixel-fade motif top-right; muted footer +
   slide number.

4. **Callout**: a full-width lime bar with bold dark-green centered text and a
   pixel-fade, used to punch a single key message (e.g. a one-line takeaway).

---

## Pixel motifs (the signature element)

- **Skyline** — divide the width into ~30 columns; each column is a vertical bar
  with a quantised (blocky) random height and a random colour from the accent
  palette; leave a small gap between bars; scatter a few tiny gray "window"
  dots. Seed the RNG so output is deterministic.
- **Pixel-fade** — a small grid of square cells (e.g. 3×6) in tints of one
  accent colour where the draw probability rises toward the solid edge, giving a
  dissolving/pixelating transition. Used on the right end of the lime bar and
  top-right of content slides.

Both are drawn as plain filled rectangles via `python-pptx` — no images needed.

---

## How to apply it

The reference implementation is **`presentation/generate_pptx.py`**, a
dependency-light renderer (only `python-pptx`) that parses a markdown deck and
emits the styled `.pptx`. Restyling a deck means editing the theme constants and
slide builders in that script — the markdown content stays the source of truth.

### Markdown grammar understood by the renderer
- Slides separated by a line that is only `---`.
- First slide starts with `# ` → **hero**; others `## ` → content.
- `^ TEXT` → eyebrow (ALL-CAPS label).
- `### TEXT` → lead / subtitle.
- `- ` / `  - ` → bullet / sub-bullet.
- `| a | b |` pipe tables → styled dark table (skip the `|---|` row).
- `:::pillars` … `:::` block, each line `Label | Sub-label` → **pillars** layout.
- `>> TEXT` → **lime outcome / callout bar**.
- `> TEXT` → speaker notes (narrative).
- Inline `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `` `code` `` are honoured.

### Generate & verify
```bash
source .venv/bin/activate          # has python-pptx installed
python presentation/generate_pptx.py
# visual check (LibreOffice + poppler):
soffice --headless --convert-to pdf --outdir /tmp/_p presentation/*.pptx
pdftoppm -png -r 70 /tmp/_p/*.pdf /tmp/_p/slide   # inspect the PNGs
```

Verification checklist: every slide on a black canvas; hero shows the skyline;
each slide carries speaker notes; tables use the accent header + dark zebra;
PASS values render green; the lime bar shows its pixel-fade.

### Reuse on a new deck
Point the renderer at a different markdown source (same grammar) and keep the
theme constants. Adjust only the accent palette if a customer brand colour is
required; keep the black canvas and pixel motifs to preserve the look.
